name: Enterprise Secrets Audit
description: Audit and manage repository and organization secrets used in GitHub Actions to ensure security and compliance.

steps.title: List repository secrets
steps.description: View all secrets configured for the current repository.

steps.title: List organization secrets
steps.description: View secrets available at the organization level.

steps.title: Set a new repository secret
steps.description: Create or update a secret for GitHub Actions.
steps.warning: Secret values are write-only. You cannot read them back after setting them.
steps.tip: For multi-line secrets or files, use: gh secret set KEY < secret-file.txt

steps.title: List environment-scoped secrets
steps.description: View secrets scoped to a specific deployment environment.

steps.title: Remove a secret
steps.description: Delete a secret that is no longer needed.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Enterprise Secrets Audit
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Repository Secrets ==="
gh secret list 2>/dev/null || echo "No repository secrets or insufficient permissions"

echo ""
echo "=== Environment Secrets ==="
for env in production staging development; do
  echo "--- $env ---"
  gh secret list --env "$env" 2>/dev/null || echo "No secrets or environment not found"
done

echo ""
echo "=== Secret Management Commands ==="
echo "Set:    gh secret set KEY --body 'value'"
echo "File:   gh secret set KEY < file.txt"
echo "Delete: gh secret delete KEY"
echo "Env:    gh secret set KEY --env production --body 'value'"
echo "Org:    gh secret set KEY --org <org> --body 'value'"

faq.question: Can I audit who accessed a secret?
faq.answer: GitHub does not log individual secret reads. You can see which workflows use secrets by searching workflow files. For audit logs, GitHub Enterprise Cloud provides organization-level audit logs.

faq.question: How do I rotate secrets safely?
faq.answer: Set the new secret value with 'gh secret set'. The next workflow run will use the new value. Revoke the old credential in the external service after confirming the new one works.
